items.summary: Extend the backend Motoko actor to support admin-only operations gated to principal ID `zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb`: get all users with activity info, delete a user, ban/unban a user, suspend/unsuspend a user; delete any book regardless of owner; approve or reject a book (set approval status); feature/unfeature a book on the homepage; get all uploaded PDFs with metadata; delete or replace a PDF; get storage usage stats and enforce a configurable max upload size; get and set platform settings (site name, logo URL, announcement banner text, maintenance mode toggle); force-logout a user (invalidate session marker); get analytics data (total users, total books, total uploads, most-viewed books, most-bookmarked books, new users and books over time). All admin endpoints must verify the caller's principal matches the hardcoded admin principal and trap/return an error otherwise.

Build a complete Admin Dashboard page at the `/admin` route, protected by the existing `AdminGuard` component. The dashboard must contain clearly separated sections reachable via a sidebar or tab navigation: Overview/Analytics, User Management, Book Management, Storage Control, Platform Settings, and Security. The UI must be clean, modern, and fully mobile-responsive.

Implement the Analytics/Overview section of the Admin Dashboard. Display stat cards for: total registered users, total books, total uploads, total approved books, total pending books. Display a list of the most-viewed books and most-bookmarked books. Display most-active users by upload count.

Implement the User Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a paginated or scrollable table of all registered users with columns: display name, principal ID, role, status (active/banned/suspended), books uploaded, join date. Provide action buttons per row to: delete user, ban/unban user, suspend/unsuspend user. Confirm destructive actions with a confirmation dialog before calling the backend.

Implement the Book Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a searchable, filterable list of all books (including pending and approved) with cover thumbnail, title, author, genre, status (approved/pending/rejected), featured flag, and view count. Provide per-book actions: edit (title, description, genre, cover URL), delete, approve, reject, feature/unfeature. The admin upload form should reuse or mirror the existing Upload page form.

Implement the Storage Control section of the Admin Dashboard. Display aggregate storage usage (total bytes used across all PDFs) and the configured maximum upload size. Show a list of all uploaded PDF entries with file name, associated book title, uploader, file size, and upload date. Provide a delete button per PDF entry and a global control to update the maximum allowed upload size.

Implement the Platform Settings section of the Admin Dashboard. Provide an editable form with fields for: site name, logo URL, homepage announcement banner text (with an enable/disable toggle), and a maintenance mode toggle switch. Saving the form calls the backend setPlatformSettings endpoint. When maintenance mode is enabled, non-admin users visiting the frontend should see a maintenance page instead of the normal app.

Implement the Security section of the Admin Dashboard. Display a list of currently banned and suspended users with their status and the ability to unban/unsuspend from this view. Add a searchable account-blocking form where the admin can enter a principal ID to block (ban) that account. Display a platform activity log showing recent events: new user registrations, book uploads, and books removed — with timestamps and actor principal.
